feeds.title: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics
feeds.tldr: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics currently has 4 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing", "Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers", and "TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data".
highlights: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing
Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers
TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data

feeds.title: Nature Communications
feeds.tldr: The digest features methodological innovations like a framework for secure cross-institutional health data analysis and AI applications in protein design and plant phenotyping. Biological insights include lung progenitor dynamics and oncogene-induced replication stress mechanisms. Environmental and technological advances cover permafrost thaw prediction, scalable battery coatings, ultrafast plasma probing, and high-efficiency hybrid energy systems.
highlights: Framework enables cross-institutional EHR analysis without sharing patient-level data.
Solution-processed PV-TE hybrids achieve 50.28% efficiency through optimized heat utilization.
AI-based design reveals broad protein folds from limited primordial amino acid libraries.

items.title: Representation learning to advance multi-institutional studies with electronic health record data from US and France
items.tldr: A framework harmonizes electronic health record data across hospitals using medical knowledge, large language models, and graph learning, enabling cross-institutional analysis without sharing patient data.

items.title: Hippo signaling differentially regulates distal progenitor subpopulations and their transitional states to construct the mammalian lungs
items.tldr: Distal SOX9+ lung progenitors control lung size via bifurcation and undergo transitional states to generate conducting airway and alveolar cell types.

items.title: Permafrost landsystems define regional variability in climate change effects on northern environments
items.tldr: Permafrost landsystems, formed by landform-ecosystem interactions and varying with geological and climatic legacies, predict regional thaw consequences.

items.title: Mechanofusion-derived cathode composite microstructures with scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings for solid state batteries
items.tldr: Solvent-free mechanofusion creates mixed conducting matrix coatings on cathode particles for solid-state batteries, with coating quality linked to mixing stresses for scalability.

items.title: A highly limited amino acid library from asteroid Bennu yields wide-ranging protein folds
items.tldr: AI-based protein design shows that small, primordial amino acid libraries constrained by asteroid Bennu data can form a wide range of protein folds.

items.title: Probing ultrafast heating and ionization dynamics in solid density plasmas with time-resolved resonant X-ray absorption and emission
items.tldr: Combining resonant X-ray emission spectroscopy and absorption imaging with an XFEL probe achieves sub-picosecond resolution of solid-density copper plasma dynamics.

items.title: Solution-processed photovoltaic and thermoelectric hybrid systems with efficiency exceeding 50%
items.tldr: Solution-processed photovoltaic-thermoelectric hybrid systems optimized for heat utilization and current matching experimentally demonstrate a maximum efficiency of 50.28%.

items.title: A conversational multi-agent AI system for automated plant phenotyping
items.tldr: A conversational multi-agent AI system integrates large language models with tools for phenotype extraction, visualization, and model training to automate plant phenotyping and enhance accessibility.

items.title: Distinct mechanisms of replication stress induced by oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal
items.tldr: Oncogenic RAS induces replication fork stalling through ROS-driven replisome architecture alterations, leading to R-loop-dependent fork reversal.

items.title: Influence of the broadly neutralizing antibody VRC01 on HIV breakthrough virus populations in antibody-mediated prevention trials
items.tldr: Deep sequencing of HIV prevention trials shows high multilineage infections (38%) and demonstrates that VRC01 does not alter the transmission bottleneck.

feeds.title: Nature Computational Science
feeds.tldr: Nature Computational Science currently has 2 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "Universal restoration of medical images" and "Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data".
highlights: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data

items.title: Universal restoration of medical images
items.tldr: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00975-1 A self-supervised foundation model, HorusEye, learns realistic noise directly from X-ray scans and enables robust tomography restoration across diverse modalities, s…

items.title: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data
items.tldr: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00977-z A benchmark to evaluate spatial alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics shows that the best approach is dataset dependent and offers actionable guidance for th…

feeds.title: Nature Health
feeds.tldr: This Nature Health digest includes a paper discussing GLP-1 receptor drugs in longevity research. The title advocates for rigorous testing of agonists, while the body text classifies antagonists as transitional therapeutics needing more evidence. The paper emphasizes assessing direct and indirect effects for these drugs.
highlights: GLP-1 receptor antagonists classified as transitional longevity therapeutics
Need for more evidence on direct and indirect effects
Title calls for rigorous testing of agonists

items.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
items.tldr: The paper's title argues for testing GLP-1 receptor agonists as longevity therapeutics. The body text states that GLP-1 receptor antagonists are transitional but require additional evidence to evaluate their effects.

feeds.title: Nature Medicine
feeds.tldr: This digest features a study identifying a new monkeypox virus lineage and co-circulation risks in the Republic of the Congo. Another analysis across 34 countries links social exposures to faster functional brain aging and physical exposures to faster structural brain aging. Finally, target product profiles are outlined for Alzheimer's therapies to delay or prevent symptomatic onset.
highlights: Monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 found in Congo with co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb.
Social exposures accelerate functional brain aging; physical exposures accelerate structural brain aging.
Benchmarks proposed for Alzheimer's treatments to delay or prevent clinical symptoms.

items.title: Evidence of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clade Ia, Ib and clade IIb
items.tldr: Whole-genome phylogenetic analyses identified a case of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb, emphasizing improved surveillance due to recombination risks.

items.title: The exposome of brain aging across 34 countries
items.tldr: Exposome analyses across 34 countries showed that social exposures are associated with faster functional brain aging, while physical exposures are associated with faster structural brain aging.

items.title: Target product profiles for treatments to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease
items.tldr: Authors outline target product profiles for therapies designed to delay or prevent the onset of clinical symptoms of Alzheimer's disease to accelerate development and provide benchmarks.

feeds.title: Nature Microbiology
feeds.tldr: This digest includes studies on microbial diversity expansion through unbinned contigs, a mechanism blocking plasmid transfer via endonucleases, and lipid-mediated impairment of immune responses in tuberculosis. The first paper reveals vast hidden prokaryotic diversity in global metagenomes, the second describes YokF endonuclease inhibiting nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange, and the third shows cholesterol accumulation in TB granulomas disrupts macrophage-T cell interactions.
highlights: Unbinned contigs in metagenomes uncover hundreds of thousands of new bacterial and archaeal species, with soils and aquatic environments as hotspots.
YokF endonuclease provides a donor-controlled block to plasmid transfer via nanotubes in Bacillus subtilis, with homologues in Gram-positive bacteria.
Cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions, compromising anti-tuberculosis immunity.

items.title: Unbinned contigs expand known diversity in the global microbiome
items.tldr: Analysis of 92,187 metagenomes predicts ~705,000 bacterial and ~27,000 archaeal species-level clades, mostly hidden in unbinned contigs, and identifies soils and aquatic environments as hotspots of discoverable lineages.

items.title: A family of endonucleases blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange
items.tldr: YokF endonuclease limits plasmid transfer to recipient bacteria via nanotubes in Bacillus subtilis, with functional homologues found across Gram-positive bacterial species.

items.title: Lipid accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas inhibits macrophage–CD4 + T cell interactions and infection control
items.tldr: Spatial transcriptomics and microscopy reveal that infection-driven cholesterol accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions and anti-tuberculosis immunity.
